description: UI/UX Sprint Session 3 — Campaign management redesign, chart x-axis smart formatting, settings mobile UI, cost/analytics empty states, Apify color, mobile campaign icon

userStories.title: Campaign Management Table — Desktop Redesign
userStories.description: Redesign the campaign management table on desktop: improve status badge design with pill-style indicators, reposition group expand/collapse chevron arrows to be less cluttered, add cleaner column spacing, and modernize the overall card layout for the Campaign Management section.
acceptanceCriteria: Status badges use refined pill design with dot indicators
Group expand chevrons are cleanly positioned outside the name column
Table header uses cleaner typography and spacing
Overall card has better visual hierarchy
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Chart X-Axis — Smart Date Tick Formatting
userStories.description: Improve x-axis date rendering for all time-series charts (click rate, reply rate, email sends, opt-out rate via TimeSeriesChart, and daily LLM cost via DailyCostChart). When the date range grows, the current interval='preserveStartEnd' causes overlapping or illegible labels. Implement a smart tick formatter that adapts label density and format based on the total number of data points: <=7 days shows 'Mon DD', 8-30 shows every ~5th tick, 31-90 shows 'Mon' only, 90+ shows 'Mon YY'.
acceptanceCriteria: TimeSeriesChart x-axis adapts tick interval and format based on data length
DailyCostChart x-axis uses the same smart formatter
Labels never overlap even at 90+ day ranges
Short ranges (<=7 days) still show full date labels
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: General Settings Tab — Mobile-Native Layout
userStories.description: The GeneralSettingsTab is desktop-only with side-by-side label+input rows using flex items-center justify-between with fixed w-64 inputs. On mobile these don't stack. Convert to responsive layout: labels stack above inputs on mobile, inputs become full-width, section headers remain, workspace info grid stacks vertically.
acceptanceCriteria: Setting rows stack vertically on mobile (label above, input below)
Inputs are full-width on mobile, w-64 on md+
Section headers render properly on small screens
Workspace info grid stacks single-column on mobile
Save button is accessible on mobile
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Campaign Management Card Stack — Mobile Redesign
userStories.description: Apply the same design improvements from UIS3-001 (desktop) to the mobile CampaignManagementCardStack: improve status badge pill design, refine group card header styling, better spacing on campaign cards, and cleaner overflow menu positioning.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile campaign cards use refined status badge design matching desktop
GroupCard headers have better visual hierarchy
Card spacing and padding is optimized for touch targets
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Mobile Bottom Nav — Add Campaign Quick Create Icon
userStories.description: The CampaignQuickCreate feature (Layers icon with dropdown for campaign groups + create) exists in the desktop top navbar but has no mobile equivalent. Add the CampaignQuickCreate component to the mobile header (MobileHeader) so mobile users can access campaign group filtering and creation.
acceptanceCriteria: CampaignQuickCreate icon appears in MobileHeader alongside bell + profile
Dropdown works correctly on mobile viewport
Campaign group selection and create modal function on mobile
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: DonutChart — Enhanced Empty State for Cost Cards
userStories.description: The DonutChart empty state (for Cost by Provider and Cost by Model) is too sparse — just a PieChart icon and one line of text. Enhance with: a more detailed illustration area, a subtle dashed ring placeholder, action hint text, and a muted color palette that makes the empty state feel intentional rather than broken.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty state has a dashed ring placeholder simulating a donut chart
Icon is larger and more prominent
Description text is more helpful with action guidance
Visual weight matches the non-empty chart state
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Analytics Page — Enhanced Empty States for Tables
userStories.description: Model Usage Details and Per-Sender Breakdown tables show minimal empty states ('No model usage data available' / 'No sender data available yet'). These are too bare. Add icon illustrations, descriptive helper text explaining what data will appear, and maintain the column headers so the structure is clear.
acceptanceCriteria: Model Usage Details empty state has an icon and descriptive text
Per-Sender Breakdown empty state has an icon and descriptive text
Column headers remain visible in empty state
Empty states have visual weight matching the populated state
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Apify Color — Change from Teal to Red
userStories.description: In lib/constants.ts, the CHART_COLORS.apify value is '#00d1b2' (teal). Change it to a red color to differentiate it from other providers in the donut charts.
acceptanceCriteria: CHART_COLORS.apify is changed to a red color value
Apify appears red in Cost by Provider and Cost by Model donut charts
npx tsc --noEmit passes
